knowledge.title: Current Implementation Status (21 March 2026)
knowledge.content:
## Backend (FastAPI)

WORKING: Auth (Mock SSO), RBAC (full CRUD), Audit Trail, Buying Steps 1-7, Schedule CRUD, Scheduler Trigger with locking, Seed Data (12 countries, 11 OpCo, 4 demo users, 5 roles)

PARTIAL: Step 0 (placeholder), Step 2 (eligibility gaps in sales_type/intercompany fields), Step 4 (hardcoded 50K credit limit), Step 5 (no Warsaw API), Reporting (only temp-invoices endpoint)

MISSING: Notifications, Backup/Restore, OpCo management endpoints, Start dates management, Customer endpoints, Credit limit sync, Data quality validation

Empty module directories: credit_limits, customers, itrp, jobs, maintenance, notifications, opco, startdates

## Frontend (Next.js 14 + Ant Design 5)

WORKING: Login, Dashboard (7-step orchestration), Admin (users/roles/audit), ITRP auto-buy preview, Schedule config

PARTIAL: ITRP settings (start date placeholder), ITRP maintenance (stub tabs)

MISSING: Reporting page, My Customers page, XTRP page

## Storage

Firestore: 13 collections (users, roles, assignments, audit_trail, purchase_records, countries, opcos, opco_schedules, buy_locks, credit_limit_breaches, invoice_priorities, _unique_emails, _unique_role_names)

BigQuery: dataset itrp_warsaw_export with tables: temp_invoice (source), purchase_records, autobuy_job_executions, autobuy_invoice_results, rpt_purchased_invoices, rpt_settlement_report_economic, rpt_settlement_report_legal

versions.content:
## Backend (FastAPI)

WORKING: Auth (Mock SSO), RBAC (full CRUD), Audit Trail, Buying Steps 1-7, Schedule CRUD, Scheduler Trigger with locking, Seed Data (12 countries, 11 OpCo, 4 demo users, 5 roles)

PARTIAL: Step 0 (placeholder), Step 2 (eligibility gaps in sales_type/intercompany fields), Step 4 (hardcoded 50K credit limit), Step 5 (no Warsaw API), Reporting (only temp-invoices endpoint)

MISSING: Notifications, Backup/Restore, OpCo management endpoints, Start dates management, Customer endpoints, Credit limit sync, Data quality validation

Empty module directories: credit_limits, customers, itrp, jobs, maintenance, notifications, opco, startdates

## Frontend (Next.js 14 + Ant Design 5)

WORKING: Login, Dashboard (7-step orchestration), Admin (users/roles/audit), ITRP auto-buy preview, Schedule config

PARTIAL: ITRP settings (start date placeholder), ITRP maintenance (stub tabs)

MISSING: Reporting page, My Customers page, XTRP page

## Storage

Firestore: 13 collections (users, roles, assignments, audit_trail, purchase_records, countries, opcos, opco_schedules, buy_locks, credit_limit_breaches, invoice_priorities, _unique_emails, _unique_role_names)

BigQuery: dataset itrp_warsaw_export with tables: temp_invoice (source), purchase_records, autobuy_job_executions, autobuy_invoice_results, rpt_purchased_invoices, rpt_settlement_report_economic, rpt_settlement_report_legal

knowledge.title: IMP_02 — Purchase Priorities + RBAC Full Spec
knowledge.content: Full implementation spec from track_changes/.documentation/plan/IMP_02.md. Covers: (1) Purchase Priorities — BR-P01..P06, Option B data model (invoice_priorities collection), Step 3 pipeline integration, UI requirements (up/down, lock, cut-off). (2) RBAC — 5 roles (itrp_manager, back_office, mid_office, opco, global_finance_it) with per-page permission matrix, country scoping Option B, Firestore data model (users, roles, user_role_assignments). (3) Backend enforcement — check_country_access per endpoint, HTTP 403, server-side only. (4) Frontend guards — route-level 403, menu visibility, PermissionGate component. (5) Audit trail — 19 action types, immutable Firestore + BQ dual-write. (6) 15 open items OI-01..OI-15. Trust: HIGH.

versions.content: Full implementation spec from track_changes/.documentation/plan/IMP_02.md. Covers: (1) Purchase Priorities — BR-P01..P06, Option B data model (invoice_priorities collection), Step 3 pipeline integration, UI requirements (up/down, lock, cut-off). (2) RBAC — 5 roles (itrp_manager, back_office, mid_office, opco, global_finance_it) with per-page permission matrix, country scoping Option B, Firestore data model (users, roles, user_role_assignments). (3) Backend enforcement — check_country_access per endpoint, HTTP 403, server-side only. (4) Frontend guards — route-level 403, menu visibility, PermissionGate component. (5) Audit trail — 19 action types, immutable Firestore + BQ dual-write. (6) 15 open items OI-01..OI-15. Trust: HIGH.

knowledge.title: REQ-PP01: Priority reorder UI — up/down controls
knowledge.content: Users with ITRP Manager or Back Office role can adjust invoice priority up/down. Frontend must have up/down arrow buttons or drag-and-drop. Disabled on first/last row. BR-P02.

versions.content: Users with ITRP Manager or Back Office role can adjust invoice priority up/down. Frontend must have up/down arrow buttons or drag-and-drop. Disabled on first/last row. BR-P02.

knowledge.title: REQ-PP02: Priority read-only lock during auto-buy
knowledge.content: Priority UI and API must be locked (read-only) when auto-buy job is running for the selected country. Changes after job start have no effect (BR-P03). Requires checking buy_locks collection.

versions.content: Priority UI and API must be locked (read-only) when auto-buy job is running for the selected country. Changes after job start have no effect (BR-P03). Requires checking buy_locks collection.

knowledge.title: REQ-PP03: Priority sorting in buying pipeline Step 3
knowledge.content: Buying Service Step 3 reads purchase_priority_overrides, sorts eligible batch before credit limit check. Invoice-level priority takes precedence over customer-level. Default order: document_date ASC, open_amount DESC.

versions.content: Buying Service Step 3 reads purchase_priority_overrides, sorts eligible batch before credit limit check. Invoice-level priority takes precedence over customer-level. Default order: document_date ASC, open_amount DESC.

knowledge.title: REQ-RBAC01: Route-level 403 guards per page
knowledge.content: Every frontend page must have PermissionGate wrapper with 403 fallback. IMP_02 section 8.3 defines navigation matrix per role. Currently only Admin page has 403 guard.

versions.content: Every frontend page must have PermissionGate wrapper with 403 fallback. IMP_02 section 8.3 defines navigation matrix per role. Currently only Admin page has 403 guard.

knowledge.title: REQ-RBAC02: Menu visibility per role
knowledge.content: Navigation menu items filtered by user permissions. OpCo sees only Dashboard, Reporting, My Customers. Mid Office does not see Settings, Maintenance, Manual Buy, Admin. XTRP dead link must be removed.

versions.content: Navigation menu items filtered by user permissions. OpCo sees only Dashboard, Reporting, My Customers. Mid Office does not see Settings, Maintenance, Manual Buy, Admin. XTRP dead link must be removed.

knowledge.title: REQ-RBAC03: RBAC permission matrix per page
knowledge.content: Full permission matrix for 5 roles across 10 pages/features defined in IMP_02 sections 3.1-3.10. Dashboard ON/OFF toggle requires maintenance:write. Manual Buy requires purchase:execute. Settings requires maintenance:read. Audit Trail visible only to ITRP Manager and GFI.

versions.content: Full permission matrix for 5 roles across 10 pages/features defined in IMP_02 sections 3.1-3.10. Dashboard ON/OFF toggle requires maintenance:write. Manual Buy requires purchase:execute. Settings requires maintenance:read. Audit Trail visible only to ITRP Manager and GFI.

knowledge.title: REQ-CS01: Back Office country scoping
knowledge.content: Back Office users should have country-scoped access (Option B), same model as ITRP Manager. Each Back Office assigned to 1+ countries. Decision D-017.

versions.content: Back Office users should have country-scoped access (Option B), same model as ITRP Manager. Each Back Office assigned to 1+ countries. Decision D-017.

knowledge.title: REQ-CS02: My Customers dynamic country list
knowledge.content: My Customers page must use allowedCountries() from auth store instead of hardcoded country list. OpCo user scoped to PL should see only PL.

versions.content: My Customers page must use allowedCountries() from auth store instead of hardcoded country list. OpCo user scoped to PL should see only PL.

knowledge.title: REQ-AUDIT01: Log unauthorized access attempts
knowledge.content: All denied access attempts must be logged to audit trail (REQ-018). Add ACCESS_DENIED action to ALLOWED_ACTIONS. Log in require_permission() and check_country_access() on 403.

versions.content: All denied access attempts must be logged to audit trail (REQ-018). Add ACCESS_DENIED action to ALLOWED_ACTIONS. Log in require_permission() and check_country_access() on 403.

knowledge.title: REQ-LOCK01: Manual Buy disabled during auto-buy
knowledge.content: Manual Buy button disabled when auto-buy lock active for selected country. Frontend checks lock status endpoint. Shows warning message. REQ-010a.

versions.content: Manual Buy button disabled when auto-buy lock active for selected country. Frontend checks lock status endpoint. Shows warning message. REQ-010a.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCHEMA01: Firestore schema set_at field
knowledge.content: invoice_priorities schema in firestore_schema.py missing set_at field. Field is already written by router but not documented in schema.

versions.content: invoice_priorities schema in firestore_schema.py missing set_at field. Field is already written by router but not documented in schema.

knowledge.title: BR-P01: Priority at invoice or customer/debtor level
knowledge.content: Priority can be set at invoice level OR customer/debtor level. Invoice-level takes precedence over customer-level when both exist.

versions.content: Priority can be set at invoice level OR customer/debtor level. Invoice-level takes precedence over customer-level when both exist.

knowledge.title: BR-P04: No priority = default order
knowledge.content: When no manual priority is set, invoices processed in system default order: document_date ASC, open_amount DESC as tiebreaker. Implemented in Step 3.

versions.content: When no manual priority is set, invoices processed in system default order: document_date ASC, open_amount DESC as tiebreaker. Implemented in Step 3.

knowledge.title: BR-P05: Credit limit iterates in priority order
knowledge.content: Credit limit check (Step 4) iterates batch in priority order. Higher priority invoices evaluated first, consuming available headroom. Priority determines WHICH invoices fit under limit.

versions.content: Credit limit check (Step 4) iterates batch in priority order. Higher priority invoices evaluated first, consuming available headroom. Priority determines WHICH invoices fit under limit.

knowledge.title: BR-P06: Priorities persist across runs
knowledge.content: Priority settings persist in Application Datastore and apply to future Auto-Buy runs unless manually changed. Do NOT reset after each run.

versions.content: Priority settings persist in Application Datastore and apply to future Auto-Buy runs unless manually changed. Do NOT reset after each run.

knowledge.title: RBAC: 5 roles with server-side enforcement
knowledge.content: 5 roles: itrp_manager (full), back_office (execute), mid_office (read/TBC), opco (own data only), global_finance_it (oversight+admin). Frontend gates are UX only — backend is sole security boundary. Firestore lookup per request.

versions.content: 5 roles: itrp_manager (full), back_office (execute), mid_office (read/TBC), opco (own data only), global_finance_it (oversight+admin). Frontend gates are UX only — backend is sole security boundary. Firestore lookup per request.

knowledge.title: RBAC: Country scoping Option B
knowledge.content: Each ITRP Manager and Back Office user assigned to 1+ countries. GFI bypasses country filter. OpCo scoped to own company. Country enforcement on every data endpoint.

versions.content: Each ITRP Manager and Back Office user assigned to 1+ countries. GFI bypasses country filter. OpCo scoped to own company. Country enforcement on every data endpoint.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH01: Schedule page — GET config filtered by role
knowledge.content: GET /api/v1/schedule/config returns only countries the calling user is assigned to (ITRP Manager) or all countries (GFI). Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #1.

versions.content: GET /api/v1/schedule/config returns only countries the calling user is assigned to (ITRP Manager) or all countries (GFI). Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #1.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH02: PUT config — 403 for unassigned countries
knowledge.content: PUT /api/v1/schedule/config returns HTTP 403 for any country in request body that caller is not assigned to. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #2.

versions.content: PUT /api/v1/schedule/config returns HTTP 403 for any country in request body that caller is not assigned to. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #2.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH03: POST countries — GFI only
knowledge.content: POST /api/v1/schedule/countries returns HTTP 403 for any caller who is not Global Finance IT. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #3.

versions.content: POST /api/v1/schedule/countries returns HTTP 403 for any caller who is not Global Finance IT. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #3.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH04: Auto-buy enable requires weekday
knowledge.content: Auto-buy cannot be enabled for a country without a weekday set. API returns HTTP 400. BR-SCH-01. Source: SPEC Section 4.2.

versions.content: Auto-buy cannot be enabled for a country without a weekday set. API returns HTTP 400. BR-SCH-01. Source: SPEC Section 4.2.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH05: Running job skipped not errored
knowledge.content: A country whose auto-buy job is actively running is excluded from bulk save — reported in skipped_countries, does not produce error blocking the rest. BR-SCH-04. Source: SPEC Section 4.2.

versions.content: A country whose auto-buy job is actively running is excluded from bulk save — reported in skipped_countries, does not produce error blocking the rest. BR-SCH-04. Source: SPEC Section 4.2.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH06: Audit trail on every schedule write
knowledge.content: Every successful write produces audit trail entry with user ID, timestamp, changed fields (old+new values). BR-SCH-07. Source: SPEC Section 6.

versions.content: Every successful write produces audit trail entry with user ID, timestamp, changed fields (old+new values). BR-SCH-07. Source: SPEC Section 6.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH07: No hardcoded schedule data
knowledge.content: No country, currency, or schedule data hardcoded in application code or env vars. BR-SCH-05. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #7.

versions.content: No country, currency, or schedule data hardcoded in application code or env vars. BR-SCH-05. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #7.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH08: New country defaults auto_buy_enabled=false
knowledge.content: Adding a new country via POST defaults auto_buy_enabled to false regardless of request body value. Source: SPEC Section 4.5.

versions.content: Adding a new country via POST defaults auto_buy_enabled to false regardless of request body value. Source: SPEC Section 4.5.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH09: ISO country code validation
knowledge.content: Country codes submitted via POST validated against ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. Source: SPEC Section 4.5.

versions.content: Country codes submitted via POST validated against ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. Source: SPEC Section 4.5.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH10: Duplicate country returns 409
knowledge.content: Duplicate country codes (POST when country already exists) return HTTP 409. Source: SPEC Section 4.5.

versions.content: Duplicate country codes (POST when country already exists) return HTTP 409. Source: SPEC Section 4.5.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH11: Soft delete only — no hard deletion
knowledge.content: Removing/disabling a country uses soft delete only. No DELETE operations on countries or opco_schedule. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #11.

versions.content: Removing/disabling a country uses soft delete only. No DELETE operations on countries or opco_schedule. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Backend #11.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH12: FE — 403 redirect for unauthorized roles
knowledge.content: Page inaccessible (redirect to 403) for Back Office, Mid Office, OpCo roles. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #1.

versions.content: Page inaccessible (redirect to 403) for Back Office, Mid Office, OpCo roles. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #1.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH13: FE — ITRP Manager sees only assigned countries
knowledge.content: ITRP Manager sees only their assigned countries in the schedule table. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #2.

versions.content: ITRP Manager sees only their assigned countries in the schedule table. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #2.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH14: FE — Add Country button GFI only
knowledge.content: The Add Country button is visible only to Global Finance IT. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #4.

versions.content: The Add Country button is visible only to Global Finance IT. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #4.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH15: FE — No hardcoded country lists
knowledge.content: All country and currency data fetched from backend — no hardcoded lists in frontend code. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #5.

versions.content: All country and currency data fetched from backend — no hardcoded lists in frontend code. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #5.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH16: FE — Unsaved changes warning
knowledge.content: Unsaved changes trigger browser navigation warning. Toggling without saving does not change DB. Source: SPEC Section 3.4.

versions.content: Unsaved changes trigger browser navigation warning. Toggling without saving does not change DB. Source: SPEC Section 3.4.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH17: FE — No optimistic UI updates
knowledge.content: No optimistic UI updates. Displayed values only change after confirmed HTTP 200 response. Source: SPEC Section 3.4.

versions.content: No optimistic UI updates. Displayed values only change after confirmed HTTP 200 response. Source: SPEC Section 3.4.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH18: FE — Running job disables editing with tooltip
knowledge.content: A row where auto-buy is currently running shows all editable fields as disabled with explanatory tooltip. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #9.

versions.content: A row where auto-buy is currently running shows all editable fields as disabled with explanatory tooltip. Source: SPEC Section 8 AC Frontend #9.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH19: FE — Select All / Deselect All
knowledge.content: Select All / Deselect All toggles the Auto-Buy Enabled column for all visible rows. Source: SPEC Section 3.4.

versions.content: Select All / Deselect All toggles the Auto-Buy Enabled column for all visible rows. Source: SPEC Section 3.4.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH20: FE — Inactive countries visible but distinct
knowledge.content: Inactive countries (auto_buy OFF or not yet configured) appear in a visually distinct section below active countries. Not hidden. Source: SPEC Section 3.2.

versions.content: Inactive countries (auto_buy OFF or not yet configured) appear in a visually distinct section below active countries. Not hidden. Source: SPEC Section 3.2.

knowledge.title: REQ-SCH21: FE — Validation rules FV-01 to FV-04
knowledge.content: Frontend validates: FV-01 Purchase Day required if ON, FV-02 Start Date valid date, FV-03 Start Date not >10yr future, FV-04 File Basis required if ON (note: file_day_basis is obsolete per ARCH-007). Source: SPEC Section 3.5.

versions.content: Frontend validates: FV-01 Purchase Day required if ON, FV-02 Start Date valid date, FV-03 Start Date not >10yr future, FV-04 File Basis required if ON (note: file_day_basis is obsolete per ARCH-007). Source: SPEC Section 3.5.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB01: Step 0 — Data quality pre-buy validation
knowledge.content: Before purchase, validate invoice data quality: non-null invoice_id, valid date, positive open_amount, matching currency, existing legal_entity_code, existing customer_id. Exact rules and failure behavior TBD in separate story. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 0.

versions.content: Before purchase, validate invoice data quality: non-null invoice_id, valid date, positive open_amount, matching currency, existing legal_entity_code, existing customer_id. Exact rules and failure behavior TBD in separate story. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 0.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB02: Step 1 — Fetch latest week invoices from BQ
knowledge.content: Retrieve invoices from BigQuery for the current week and country. Must use latest file for current week, never previous week. If no data arrived, abort and notify FTC. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 1.

versions.content: Retrieve invoices from BigQuery for the current week and country. Must use latest file for current week, never previous week. If no data arrived, abort and notify FTC. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 1.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB03: Step 2 — 8 eligibility rules in sequence
knowledge.content: Apply 8 eligibility rules in priority order: (1) future-dated=hard block, (2) previously purchased=hard block, (3) before start date=hard block, (4) OpCo Off=configurable, (5) OpCo New=auto-disabled, (6) sales type not Economic/Legal=excluded, (7) intercompany=excluded, (8) credit limit breach=soft block. First match removes invoice. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 2 + Eligibility Rules table.

versions.content: Apply 8 eligibility rules in priority order: (1) future-dated=hard block, (2) previously purchased=hard block, (3) before start date=hard block, (4) OpCo Off=configurable, (5) OpCo New=auto-disabled, (6) sales type not Economic/Legal=excluded, (7) intercompany=excluded, (8) credit limit breach=soft block. First match removes invoice. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 2 + Eligibility Rules table.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB04: Step 3 — Manual priority ordering
knowledge.content: Reorder eligible invoice batch by user-set priorities (invoice-level or customer/debtor-level). No priority = default order. Priority changes after job start have no effect on current run. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 3.

versions.content: Reorder eligible invoice batch by user-set priorities (invoice-level or customer/debtor-level). No priority = default order. Priority changes after job start have no effect on current run. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 3.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB05: Step 4 — Credit limit check with FTC pause
knowledge.content: Check cumulative exposure per customer against credit limit. Breach: PAUSE auto-buy, email FTC Manager, await confirmation. FTC confirms=include+audit, FTC declines=exclude+flag. Only step where auto-buy is not fully unattended. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 4.

versions.content: Check cumulative exposure per customer against credit limit. Breach: PAUSE auto-buy, email FTC Manager, await confirmation. FTC confirms=include+audit, FTC declines=exclude+flag. Only step where auto-buy is not fully unattended. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 4.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB06: Step 5 — All-or-nothing purchase execution
knowledge.content: Execute purchase batch. ANY invoice fails = roll back ENTIRE job, delete all records from this run, revert to pre-purchase state. Success = write purchase_records to BQ, update status flags. Status flags: Purchased, Purchase Failed, Incomplete Purchase. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 5.

versions.content: Execute purchase batch. ANY invoice fails = roll back ENTIRE job, delete all records from this run, revert to pre-purchase state. Success = write purchase_records to BQ, update status flags. Status flags: Purchased, Purchase Failed, Incomplete Purchase. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 5.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB07: Step 6 — Persist job execution record
knowledge.content: Write permanent job execution record to BQ regardless of outcome: job_id, schedule_id, country, start_time, end_time, status, invoices_attempted/succeeded/failed. Plus per-invoice results table: outcome, error_code, error_message, http_status. Written even on full rollback. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 6.

versions.content: Write permanent job execution record to BQ regardless of outcome: job_id, schedule_id, country, start_time, end_time, status, invoices_attempted/succeeded/failed. Plus per-invoice results table: outcome, error_code, error_message, http_status. Written even on full rollback. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 6.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB08: Step 7 — Update reporting data on success
knowledge.content: After successful run, materialise reporting tables: Settlement Report (Economic+Legal separate), ITRP Report (monthly aggregate), Purchased Invoices view. Only on success — not after rollback. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 7.

versions.content: After successful run, materialise reporting tables: Settlement Report (Economic+Legal separate), ITRP Report (monthly aggregate), Purchased Invoices view. Only on success — not after rollback. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 7.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB09: Step 8 — Email notifications
knowledge.content: Success: 2 separate emails (Economic, Legal) with purchase summary. Failure: 1 consolidated email per job run with date/time, country, attempted/succeeded/failed counts, list of failed invoices+reasons. Key rule: one email per job on failure, not per invoice. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 8.

versions.content: Success: 2 separate emails (Economic, Legal) with purchase summary. Failure: 1 consolidated email per job run with date/time, country, attempted/succeeded/failed counts, list of failed invoices+reasons. Key rule: one email per job on failure, not per invoice. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 8.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB10: Step 9 — Release country lock
knowledge.content: Release country-level mutex lock regardless of outcome (success or failure). Must also handle unexpected crash via timeout/TTL cleanup to prevent permanent lockout. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 9.

versions.content: Release country-level mutex lock regardless of outcome (success or failure). Must also handle unexpected crash via timeout/TTL cleanup to prevent permanent lockout. Source: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md Step 9.

knowledge.title: BR-SCH: Schedule business rules (7 rules)
knowledge.content:
BR-SCH-01: weekday mandatory when auto_buy_enabled=true
BR-SCH-02: file_day_basis mandatory when enabled (OBSOLETE per ARCH-007)
BR-SCH-03: Caller must be assigned to every country in request (ITRP Manager), GFI exempt
BR-SCH-04: Running auto-buy job = country skipped not errored
BR-SCH-05: All schedule data in DB, never in code/env vars
BR-SCH-06: Every write records updated_by + updated_at
BR-SCH-07: Every write produces audit trail entry

versions.content:
BR-SCH-01: weekday mandatory when auto_buy_enabled=true
BR-SCH-02: file_day_basis mandatory when enabled (OBSOLETE per ARCH-007)
BR-SCH-03: Caller must be assigned to every country in request (ITRP Manager), GFI exempt
BR-SCH-04: Running auto-buy job = country skipped not errored
BR-SCH-05: All schedule data in DB, never in code/env vars
BR-SCH-06: Every write records updated_by + updated_at
BR-SCH-07: Every write produces audit trail entry

knowledge.title: BR-AB: Auto-buy eligibility rules priority order
knowledge.content:
8 rules applied in sequence, first match removes invoice:
1. Future-dated (hard block, no override)
2. Previously purchased (hard block, idempotency guard)
3. Before entity start date (hard block)
4. OpCo Off (configurable — toggle back On)
5. OpCo New/re-joining (auto-disabled — ITRP Manager re-enables)
6. Sales type not Economic/Legal (manual buy override only)
7. Intercompany (manual buy override only)
8. Credit limit breach (soft block — FTC confirmation)

versions.content:

5. OpCo New/re-joining (auto-disabled — ITRP Manager re-enables)
6. Sales type not Economic/Legal (manual buy override only)
7. Intercompany (manual buy override only)
8. Credit limit breach (soft block — FTC confirmation)

knowledge.title: BR-AB-ROLLBACK: All-or-nothing purchase execution
knowledge.content: If any invoice fails during auto-buy execution, the ENTIRE job rolls back. No partial purchases. All records from this run deleted. System reverts to pre-purchase state. Single consolidated failure email sent per run. Source: Ac Autobuy AC.

versions.content: If any invoice fails during auto-buy execution, the ENTIRE job rolls back. No partial purchases. All records from this run deleted. System reverts to pre-purchase state. Single consolidated failure email sent per run. Source: Ac Autobuy AC.

knowledge.title: BR-AB-AUTODISABLE: Auto-disable events
knowledge.content:
Auto-buy automatically disabled (executed_by=SYSTEM) in 2 cases:
1. OpCo re-joins ITRP (is_new=true) — ITRP Manager re-enables
2. Backup restore executed — both auto-load and auto-buy disabled
Both: FTC email notification, audit trail entry, banner on schedule page.

knowledge.title: API-SCH: Schedule API endpoints (7 endpoints)
knowledge.content:
Base: /api/v1
GET /api/v1/schedule/config — fetch schedule for accessible countries
PUT /api/v1/schedule/config — bulk save schedule (full replacement of user-accessible rows)
PATCH /api/v1/schedule/config/{countryCode} — partial update single country
GET /api/v1/schedule/countries — list accessible countries
POST /api/v1/schedule/countries — add new country (GFI only)
GET /api/v1/schedule/start-dates — get start dates
PATCH /api/v1/schedule/start-dates/{countryCode} — update start date
All require Bearer token. Error shape: {error, message, details[]}.

Base: /api/v1
GET /api/v1/schedule/config — fetch schedule for accessible countries
PUT /api/v1/schedule/config — bulk save schedule (full replacement of user-accessible rows)
PATCH /api/v1/schedule/config/{countryCode} — partial update single country
GET /api/v1/schedule/countries — list accessible countries
POST /api/v1/schedule/countries — add new country (GFI only)
GET /api/v1/schedule/start-dates — get start dates
PATCH /api/v1/schedule/start-dates/{countryCode} — update start date
All require Bearer token. Error shape: {error, message, details[]}.

knowledge.title: ARCH-SCHEDULER: Cloud Scheduler day-based triggers
knowledge.content: 4 Cloud Scheduler jobs (Mon/Wed/Thu/Fri) at 06:00 UTC (time TBD). Each fires POST to Trigger Service. Trigger Service queries DB for countries scheduled today. No per-country jobs. Adding country = DB insert only, no infra change. New day (e.g. Tuesday) requires new Cloud Scheduler job.

versions.content: 4 Cloud Scheduler jobs (Mon/Wed/Thu/Fri) at 06:00 UTC (time TBD). Each fires POST to Trigger Service. Trigger Service queries DB for countries scheduled today. No per-country jobs. Adding country = DB insert only, no infra change. New day (e.g. Tuesday) requires new Cloud Scheduler job.

knowledge.title: ARCH-TRIGGER: Trigger Service responsibilities
knowledge.content: Trigger Service: (1) receives Cloud Scheduler event, (2) queries DB for today scheduled+enabled countries, (3) for each country: check file-completion gate, check manual buy lock, acquire country lock, initiate Buying Service. File-completion gate mechanism TBD (OI-05).

versions.content: Trigger Service: (1) receives Cloud Scheduler event, (2) queries DB for today scheduled+enabled countries, (3) for each country: check file-completion gate, check manual buy lock, acquire country lock, initiate Buying Service. File-completion gate mechanism TBD (OI-05).

knowledge.title: DATA-MODEL-SCH: opco_schedule + countries + user_country_assignments
knowledge.content:
opco_schedule: schedule_id(PK), country_code(FK), weekday, file_day_basis(OBSOLETE), auto_buy_enabled, start_date, updated_by, updated_at. One row per country_code.
countries: country_id(PK), country_code(UNIQUE), country_name, currency_code, currency_name, is_active, deleted_at(soft delete), created_at, created_by.
user_country_assignments: assignment_id(PK), user_id, country_code(FK), role, assigned_by, assigned_at, revoked_at(soft delete).

versions.content:
opco_schedule: schedule_id(PK), country_code(FK), weekday, file_day_basis(OBSOLETE), auto_buy_enabled, start_date, updated_by, updated_at. One row per country_code.
countries: country_id(PK), country_code(UNIQUE), country_name, currency_code, currency_name, is_active, deleted_at(soft delete), created_at, created_by.
user_country_assignments: assignment_id(PK), user_id, country_code(FK), role, assigned_by, assigned_at, revoked_at(soft delete).

knowledge.title: REQ-NOTIF: 6 notification event types
knowledge.content:
Email notifications required:
1. Auto-buy success Economic — separate email with invoice summary
2. Auto-buy success Legal — separate email with invoice summary
3. Auto-buy job failure — single consolidated email per run
4. Credit limit breach — email to FTC Manager with customer+amount
5. Auto-buy disabled (OpCo re-join) — email to FTC team
6. Missing data / pipeline issue — email to FTC team
Optional: in-app failure badge on page load.
Key: one failure email per job run, NOT per invoice.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB-LOCK: Country-level mutex lock
knowledge.content: Mutual exclusion between auto-buy and manual buy per country. Lock acquired before auto-buy starts, released in Step 9. Manual buy blocked (greyed out) with message while auto-buy runs. Lock must have TTL/timeout for crash recovery. Lock status exposed to frontend via auto_buy_running field.

Manual buy blocked (greyed out) with message while auto-buy runs. Lock must have TTL/timeout for crash recovery. Lock status exposed to frontend via auto_buy_running field.

knowledge.title: REQ-AB-SCHEDULE: 11 country purchase schedule
knowledge.content: Monday: BE, CA, DE, AU. Wednesday: FR, PL. Thursday: IT, NL, PT, SE. Friday: US. Japan: TBD (deferred post-go-live, target 1 Sep 2026). All configurable via DB without redeployment.
